terms.definition: The value of a digit based on its position in a number.
terms.example: In 45,213 the 5 has a value of 5,000 (thousands place).
terms.kid_friendly: Where a digit sits tells you how much it is worth.

terms.definition: A group of three digits separated by commas in a number.
terms.kid_friendly: Every three digits from the right make one period.

terms.definition: Replace a number with a nearby number that is easier to work with.
terms.example: 4,563 rounded to the nearest thousand is 5,000.
terms.kid_friendly: Change a number to the closest easy number.

terms.definition: Find an approximate answer, often by rounding first.
terms.kid_friendly: A smart guess that is close to the exact answer.

terms.definition: A way to write a number showing the value of each digit.
terms.kid_friendly: Break the number into place-value pieces.

terms.definition: Numbers that are easy to compute mentally.
terms.kid_friendly: Numbers you can divide or multiply in your head.

terms.definition: A number being added to one or more others.
terms.kid_friendly: The numbers you add together.

terms.definition: The result of addition.
terms.kid_friendly: The total after adding.

terms.definition: The result of subtraction.
terms.kid_friendly: What is left after subtracting.

terms.definition: The result of multiplication.
terms.kid_friendly: The answer when you multiply.

terms.definition: The result of division.
terms.kid_friendly: The answer when you divide.

terms.definition: The amount left over after dividing evenly.
terms.kid_friendly: The leftover piece that does not fit into equal groups.

terms.definition: A number multiplied by another to get a product.
terms.kid_friendly: Numbers you multiply together.

terms.definition: The product of a number and any whole number.
terms.kid_friendly: Skip-count answers.

terms.definition: A whole number greater than 1 with exactly two factors: 1 and itself.
terms.kid_friendly: Only 1 and itself divide it evenly.

terms.definition: A whole number greater than 1 that has more than two factors.
terms.kid_friendly: Has extra factors beyond 1 and itself.

terms.definition: Comparing two quantities by saying one is a certain number of times the other.
terms.kid_friendly: "Times as many" or "times as much".

terms.definition: Multiplying a sum by a number equals multiplying each addend and adding the products.
terms.kid_friendly: Break apart, multiply each part, then add.

terms.definition: Order does not change the sum or product.
terms.kid_friendly: Flip the order — same answer.

terms.definition: Grouping does not change the sum or product.
terms.kid_friendly: Move the parentheses — same answer.

terms.definition: Adding 0 or multiplying by 1 leaves the number unchanged.
terms.kid_friendly: Zero for adding, one for multiplying — nothing changes.

terms.definition: A number that names part of a whole or a group.
terms.kid_friendly: A piece of something.

terms.definition: The top number of a fraction; tells how many parts you have.
terms.kid_friendly: How many pieces you have.

terms.definition: The bottom number of a fraction; tells total equal parts.
terms.kid_friendly: How many pieces the whole is cut into.

terms.definition: Fractions that name the same amount.
terms.kid_friendly: Different names for the same size piece.

terms.definition: A number with a whole-number part and a fraction part.
terms.kid_friendly: A whole number plus a fraction.

terms.definition: A fraction whose numerator is greater than or equal to its denominator.
terms.kid_friendly: The top number is bigger — more than one whole.

terms.definition: A fraction with numerator 1.
terms.kid_friendly: One single equal piece.

terms.definition: A fraction whose numerator and denominator share no common factor other than 1.
terms.kid_friendly: The smallest way to write a fraction.

terms.definition: A shared multiple of the denominators of two or more fractions.
terms.example: For 1/3 and 1/4, a common denominator is 12.
terms.kid_friendly: The same bottom number so you can add or compare.

terms.definition: A common fraction used for estimating: 0, 1/4, 1/2, 3/4, 1.
terms.kid_friendly: Easy fractions you know well, used to estimate.

terms.definition: A number with a decimal point showing tenths, hundredths, etc.
terms.kid_friendly: A dot that shows parts smaller than 1.

terms.definition: The first place to the right of the decimal point.
terms.example: In 0.3, the 3 is in the tenths place.
terms.kid_friendly: One of ten equal pieces.

terms.definition: The second place to the right of the decimal point.
terms.example: In 0.07, the 7 is in the hundredths place.
terms.kid_friendly: One of a hundred equal pieces.

terms.definition: A figure formed by two rays sharing a common endpoint (vertex).
terms.example: The corner of a book is about a 90° angle.
terms.kid_friendly: The space between two lines that meet at a point.

terms.definition: An angle that measures exactly 90°.
terms.example: The corner of a square is a right angle.
terms.kid_friendly: A perfect L-shaped corner.

terms.definition: An angle less than 90°.
terms.kid_friendly: Smaller than a right angle.

terms.definition: An angle greater than 90° but less than 180°.
terms.kid_friendly: Wider than a right angle.

terms.definition: An angle that measures exactly 180°; a straight line.
terms.example: A straight line forms a 180° angle.
terms.kid_friendly: A perfectly flat line — half a full turn.

terms.definition: A straight path that extends forever in both directions.
terms.example: A line through points A and B is written AB with arrows on both ends.
terms.kid_friendly: Goes on forever in two directions.

terms.definition: Part of a line with two endpoints.
terms.example: Segment AB starts at A and ends at B.
terms.kid_friendly: A piece of a line with two stopping points.

terms.definition: Part of a line with one endpoint that goes on forever in one direction.
terms.example: Ray AB starts at A and goes through B forever.
terms.kid_friendly: Starts at a point and shoots out forever.

terms.definition: Lines in the same plane that never intersect.
terms.kid_friendly: Lines that stay the same distance apart forever.

terms.definition: Lines that intersect to form right angles.
terms.example: The letter T has perpendicular lines.
terms.kid_friendly: Lines that cross making perfect corners.

terms.definition: A line that divides a shape into two mirror-image halves.
terms.example: A square has 4 lines of symmetry.
terms.kid_friendly: Fold on this line and both sides match exactly.

terms.definition: The total distance around a shape.
terms.example: A rectangle 5 cm by 3 cm has perimeter 5+3+5+3 = 16 cm.
terms.kid_friendly: How far you walk around the edge.

terms.definition: The number of square units covering a flat shape.
terms.example: A 5×3 rectangle has area 15 square cm.
terms.kid_friendly: How much floor the shape covers.

terms.definition: A rule written with symbols to calculate something.
terms.example: Area of rectangle: A = l × w.
terms.kid_friendly: A math recipe.

terms.definition: A graph showing data with marks (X) above a number line.
terms.example: X marks above 1/4, 1/2 show how many items have each length.
terms.kid_friendly: X marks on a number line to count.
terms.visual_hint: X X  X X X  X

terms.definition: An ordered set of numbers or shapes that follows a rule.
terms.kid_friendly: Something that repeats or grows by a rule.

terms.definition: A description of how a pattern works.
terms.kid_friendly: The instruction that tells the pattern what to do next.
